title: Process Selection (Manufacturing)
description: Assesses analytical understanding of manufacturing process selection, volume–variety trade-offs, process analysis, break-even decisions, and business process reengineering.
subject: DSC2370 - Operations Management

questions.question: Why is process selection considered a strategic decision rather than an operational one?
options: Because it only affects short-term capacity decisions
Because it determines long-term cost structure, flexibility, and competitiveness
Because it focuses on daily scheduling
questions.answer: Because it determines long-term cost structure, flexibility, and competitiveness
questions.rationale: Process choice locks in fixed costs, workflow structure, and responsiveness for years.

questions.question: Which combination of process characteristics is MOST likely to result in the lowest unit processing cost?
options: Low volume, high variety, high visibility
High volume, low variety, low visibility
Low volume, low variety, high variation
questions.answer: High volume, low variety, low visibility
questions.rationale: Standardization and repetition reduce per-unit costs.

questions.question: A construction company building customized bridges best fits which process type?
questions.rationale: Each output is unique with a defined start and finish.

questions.question: Which statement best distinguishes jobbing from batch production?
options: Jobbing handles each order as a unique unit
Batch production has lower volumes than jobbing
questions.answer: Jobbing handles each order as a unique unit
questions.rationale: Batch repeats similar products; jobbing does not.

questions.question: Why does mass production lie between batch and continuous processes on the volume–variety continuum?
options: It produces discrete standardized products at high volume
It produces only one product forever
questions.answer: It produces discrete standardized products at high volume
questions.rationale: Mass production is high volume but still countable and assembled.

questions.question: Which feature is UNIQUE to continuous flow processes?
questions.rationale: Continuous processes run uninterrupted for long periods.

questions.question: In the product–process matrix, what is the main risk of operating off the diagonal?
options: Misalignment between product design and process capabilities
questions.answer: Misalignment between product design and process capabilities
questions.rationale: Mismatch increases cost and inefficiency.

questions.question: Which process characteristic increases costs MOST due to labor intensity?
questions.rationale: Customization and interaction reduce efficiency.

questions.question: Why are process flowcharts powerful tools for process improvement?
questions.rationale: Visibility enables informed redesign decisions.

questions.question: Which activity typically does NOT add value in a process flow?
questions.rationale: Movement does not transform the product.

questions.question: Throughput time increases MOST when:
questions.rationale: Little’s Law links WIP directly to throughput time.

questions.question: Which managerial decision directly reduces work-in-process without changing work content?
questions.rationale: Lower cycle time lowers WIP for a given throughput.

questions.question: Assembly charts differ from assembly drawings because they:
questions.rationale: Charts emphasize assembly logic.

questions.question: Routing sheets are MOST useful for:
questions.rationale: They specify operations, tools, and skills.

questions.question: Break-even analysis is limited because it:
options: Assumes linear cost and revenue relationships
questions.answer: Assumes linear cost and revenue relationships
questions.rationale: Real-world costs are often non-linear.

questions.question: A process with higher fixed costs but lower variable costs becomes attractive when:
questions.rationale: High volume spreads fixed costs.

questions.question: The point of indifference between two processes occurs when:
options: Total costs of both processes are equal
questions.answer: Total costs of both processes are equal
questions.rationale: This guides process switching decisions.

questions.question: Graphical break-even analysis is especially useful because it:
questions.rationale: Visual comparison aids managerial judgment.

questions.question: According to Little’s Law, Throughput Time equals ______ multiplied by Cycle Time.
questions.rationale: WIP is directly proportional to throughput time.

questions.question: The relationship between product design and process design is best described as ________.
questions.rationale: One constrains and enables the other.

questions.question: Which factors increase operational complexity?
questions.rationale: These reduce predictability and efficiency.

questions.question: Which characteristics are typical of mass production?
questions.rationale: Efficiency relies on repetition and specialization.

questions.question: Which documents support effective process planning?
questions.rationale: They define how production is executed.

questions.question: Match each process type with its defining characteristic.

matchPairs.right: Unique output with defined start and end

questions.question: Match each analysis concept with its purpose.
Match each process dimension with its impact.
Critically analyze how the volume–variety relationship influences process selection and long-term cost competitiveness.
Evaluate the role of Business Process Reengineering in manufacturing organizations and explain why it carries both high potential benefits and significant risks.
